text: Initialize a new manuscript linked to C:\Users\smsakewe\Documents\GitHub\KnowledgeVault, use the proposal in vault\syntheses\Research proposal - noise-robust sign-restricted SVARs.md, and prepare a carefully revised manuscript writing plan and next tasks.
User asked to remove the first two template-era transparency milestones from the manuscript timeline without reverting the work performed in those milestones.

User corrected the Bonhomme-Robin summary in KnowledgeVault and asked to carefully analyze the updated notes, revise the manuscript plan, add detailed tasks for analytic derivation and simulation verification of the Bonhomme-Robin-style results, and insert adversarial review tasks to catch mistakes, undefined objects, logical gaps, and weak story points.
Complete the next open project task by finishing the stale M21 transparency cleanup for the already-closed M0005 BR verification plan milestone.

Continue to the next project task, M05: derive the corrected bivariate cumulant map from scratch for u_t = B(a,b) epsilon_t + eta_t, including all second-, third-, and fourth-order cumulant equations and nuisance noise cumulants.

Continue with M06: adversarially audit the bivariate cumulant derivation before using it, checking indices, cumulant definitions, normalization, missing pure or mixed moments, and whether any moment is incorrectly called clean.

Continue with M07: prove what the Bonhomme-Robin analogy does and does not justify by showing why full BR quasi-JADE does not directly apply for the bivariate L=K=2 SVAR and documenting the manuscript object as a BR-style profiled inversion.

Create a new derivation file that derives a Drautzburg-Wright-like higher-moment approach robust to additive noise, shows higher moments or cumulants can be written as moment conditions robust to Gaussian noise while second moments are not robust, and contrasts an incorrect no-noise sign-plus-higher-moment approach with a noise-robust higher-moment approach that sacrifices second-moment information.

Update the manuscript plan to the new structure: first show noisy sign-restricted sets are biased with intuitive visualization, then show standard Drautzburg-Wright higher-moment refinements are misspecified under noise and can shrink the set falsely, then propose a robust DW-style higher-moment approach that drops second-moment restrictions, compare DW and robust sets as a robustness check, plan Monte Carlo evidence, defer applications, and remove old Bonhomme-Robin route mentions from the active paper plan and project controls.
Pick the next manuscript task after the updated paper plan and work on it.

User notes: focus only on the simultaneous SVAR, not VAR lags, to keep the paper simple; after analytical results for the J-test inversion, run a Monte Carlo overview to see how well it works before spending too much time; update tasks and plan and continue with the next goal task.
Pick the next open task and work on it in goal mode; selected M35, the early J-test Monte Carlo triage.
Continue with the next task in goal mode; selected M30 to audit the M35 J-test Monte Carlo triage before further evidence work.
Check the KnowledgeVault synthesis on sign restrictions and noise, replicate the single sign-restriction/DW noise figure, and add the robust-DW set.

User clarified that the desired figure is the KnowledgeVault two-row by three-column noise figure: top row sign restrictions, bottom row DW, columns for different noise levels, with an additional robust-DW row.
User corrected that the figure should use the corrected N-test cutoff rather than the artificial fixed J-score cutoff.

User noted that the corrected sign/DW/robust-DW grid tells the main story and requested a second similar figure with columns changing non-Gaussianity to show that robust DW depends on non-Gaussian shocks and widens when higher moments are weak.

User correctly pointed out that robust DW should widen to the whole graph when structural shocks are Gaussian, because pure robust DW uses only higher moments and no second moments; correct the non-Gaussianity figure accordingly.

User asked to make both grid figures clearly invert J tests for all rows, including the robust DW row, instead of mixing finite-sample J rows with a population score cutoff; use the intended 10 percent level and show/report whether standard DW contains true B0 under noise.
User says the corrected grid figures nicely tell the paper's story and asks to update the paper plan and next steps around them.
Pick the next manuscript task and work on it; selected M28 validation of the M0020 grid-pair story.
Work on manuscript in goal mode by picking the next task; selected M27 to formalize the robust DW accepted set and comparison diagnostic.
Work on manuscript in goal mode; pick the next task and work on it. Selected M29 finite-sample Monte Carlo evidence as the next task.
Work on manuscript in goal mode; pick the next task and work on it.
User decided that standard DW should use the standard chi-square critical values a researcher would use if unaware of residual noise; repeated/bootstrap cutoffs should be calibration audits.
Pick the next manuscript task and complete a focused manuscript work block in goal mode; selected M29 larger chi-square-primary Monte Carlo evidence.
Work on manuscript. Pick next task and work on it in /goal mode.

User flagged that the draft inserted the non-Gaussianity figure twice or in the wrong role, and requested that the first figure instead show fixed non-Gaussianity with increasing residual noise in rows.

Clarification: Figure 1 should keep the existing layout with first row sign restrictions, second row standard DW, third row robust DW, fixed structural non-Gaussianity, and columns increasing residual noise from no noise to medium noise to strong noise.
Investigate the robust-DW finite-sample power loss in the high-noise residual-noise figure, avoid V=(2,2), and find a defensible DGP or inference solution for the manuscript evidence.
Ensure the paper plan and next tasks are fully in line with the new diagonal-noise robust DW estimator, editing only if stale planning language remains.
Work on manuscript. Pick next task and work on it in /goal mode. Chosen task: M37, audit the M0030 diagonal-noise robust estimator.
Work on manuscript in goal mode; pick the next task and complete the M32 literature-positioning pass.
Show how Figure 1 looks when robust DW drops the invalid covariance anchor and uses only higher-cumulant moments.
Consider a new robust-DW covariance anchor based on E[e1 e2] as a function of B and diagonal noise variances, profile unknown noise variances with 0 < v <= 0.5, and show how Figure 1 looks.
Show Figure 1 using a covariance screen where residual noise variance is bounded relative to structural shock variance, e.g. noise has at most 50 percent of each structural shock variance.

Update the paper plan and future tasks so the variance-ratio robust DW bound is the proposal; schedule Section 2-4 formula sketches, LaTeX math-format cleanup, Figure 2 update, and new T-grid Figure 3, without doing those tasks now.
User asked Codex to work on the manuscript in goal mode, pick the next task, and complete a substantive work block.
Work on manuscript in goal mode by picking the next task; selected M40, the audit of the variance-ratio robust DW screen.

Run M42 manuscript math-delimiter cleanup, then rebuild Figure 2 with the variance-ratio robust row, add the Figure 3 sample-size grid, and rebuild validation and Monte Carlo evidence around the same variance-ratio proposal.
Incorporate the new revision with many comments by carefully rewriting the abstract, introduction, and Sections 2, 3, and 4, then review the manuscript against the spirit of the comments.
After logging in to GitHub, finish the missing GitHub milestone linkage and proceed with M34: a full adversarial scope, logic, and style review of the revised manuscript and evidence.
Insert a task to work through the user's comments on DW fourth-order moments, Section 4 noisy moment derivations, Figure 1 code, and possible unit-variance normalization.

Work on the next manuscript task in goal mode by resolving M48: verify DW fourth-order moment definitions, compare Figure 1 implementation, derive noisy truth moments, decide the normalization chart, and list rebuild implications.

Update the manuscript workflow and local skills so future scientific and mathematical claims require KnowledgeVault/raw-source or derivation provenance before entering manuscript prose, in response to the M48 failure.
Improve manuscript task creation and management so fragile scientific tasks use self-contained hand-off packets rather than thin task-board rows, and create the packet structure needed for M49.
Improve the manuscript skill so prompts like 'work on next task' read and execute task packets, and prompts like 'plan next tasks' create proper packet-backed tasks when needed.
Continue M49: redo the Drautzburg-Wright source and noisy moment audit from the task packet before relying on Section 3 or Figure 1 claims.

plan a new task: Replace the notation as we just discussed. afterwards, extend the notation of moment conditions to section 4. that is i dont want to see the kumulants in section 4, but replace them using the moment condition notation.

Work on task M53: replace the Section 3 h_i(B) notation with e_t(B) recovered-shock notation and rewrite Section 4 robust conditions as explicit moment equations rather than foregrounding cumulant notation.

User asked to plan a task that carefully derives transformed-noise moment conditions step by step at B=B0, including E[z1^2 z2], E[z1 z2 z3], E[z1^3 z2], E[z1^2 z2^2], E[z1^2 z2 z3], and E[z1 z2 z3 z4], and verifies whether the manuscript should switch from diag(B) normalization to unit-variance epsilon normalization, including whether a separate manuscript-wide update task is needed.

Work on task M54: derive transformed-noise moments step by step at B=B0, distinguish independent residual noise from Gaussian covariance-product simplifications, and decide whether the manuscript keeps diag(B)=1 or needs a unit-variance/rotation-chart rewrite task.

Update the manuscript plan: the main text must properly explain and derive the robust transformed-noise moment conditions from M54, including why they hold at B0, why the moment computation makes sense, and the distinction between full transformed-residual covariances S_ij(B) and transformed-noise covariances.

Plan a new task to evaluate whether robust fourth-cumulant moments with plug-in sample covariances, such as g1222_hat(B)=mean(z1 z2^3)-3 mean(z2^2) mean(z1 z2), can be treated with standard GMM/J-test theory, or whether the product-of-sample-moments structure requires augmented nuisance moments, delta-method weighting, code changes, or revised manuscript claims.

Work on the manuscript by picking the next task and implementing it; selected M56 to audit robust fourth-cumulant generated moments, GMM weighting, chi-square cutoffs, and evidence routing before M55 and M52.

Continue the manuscript goal with the next open task, M55: revise Section 4 so the main text explains why robust transformed-noise moment conditions hold at B0, distinguishes Omega(B) from S(B), and explains generated sample moment computation after M56.

Continue the manuscript goal with the next open task after M55: execute M52 by rebuilding standard-DW evidence with the source-correct moment menu and upgrading or calibrating the robust generated-moment statistic.
Continue the manuscript goal after M52 by executing the next open task, M47, the M25 standard-DW proof gate audit.
Improve the manuscript task workflow so each task can have a folder containing the task contract and a small outcome note with what was done and questions answered; also assess project navigability.

User asked to continue after Revision-20260610-190805, first get an overview of what needs to be done, then work in a structured way; dashboard and task board route next substantive work to M33, the manuscript-local replication wrapper.
User corrected that Revision-20260610-190805 contains general revision notes, draft edits, and inline draft comments; recover the actual branch content and work from those comments.
User asks to work on M66: resolve the unit-variance noise-ratio bound, update Section 4, and make Figures 1-3 use the resulting grid/J-test algorithm.
User asks to build the correct updated Figure 1 under the M66 unit-variance projected B-lambda GMM algorithm.
